Title: eQTLs play critical roles in regulating gene expression and identifying key regulators in rice

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is negatively correlated with starch content in flag leaves at heading stage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flag leaves at the heading stage; part of a co-regulated cluster in local eQTL hotspot L01.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTL mapping; GWAS co-localization; TWAS/contextual transcriptome-phenotype correlation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os01g02390 is one of four sn-glycerol 3-phosphate synthesis-related genes in local eQTL hotspot L01. 2. Its eQTL overlaps the GWAS locus LS01 for starch content. 3. Its expression is negatively correlated with starch content in rice flag leaves.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not provide a gene symbol or direct transgenic validation for this gene; it is implicated as a candidate mediator of starch variation through expression association.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is negatively correlated with starch content in flag leaves at heading stage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flag leaves at the heading stage; member of co-regulated Cluster 2 in hotspot L01.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTL mapping; GWAS co-localization; expression-phenotype correlation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os01g02400 is one of four sn-glycerol 3-phosphate synthesis-related genes in L01 whose eQTL overlaps the starch GWAS signal. 2. Lead SNP vg0101026223 is significant for both this gene's eQTL and starch GWAS. 3. Its expression is negatively correlated with starch content.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as representative gene in Fig. 3f for negative correlation with starch content.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is negatively correlated with starch content in flag leaves at heading stage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flag leaves at the heading stage; part of co-regulated Cluster 2 in hotspot L01.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTL mapping; GWAS co-localization; expression-phenotype correlation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os01g02420 is implicated by overlapping eQTL and GWAS signals near hotspot L01. 2. It belongs to a cluster of sn-glycerol 3-phosphate synthesis-related genes associated with starch variation. 3. Higher transcript level is associated with lower starch content.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate causal gene inferred from combined eQTL/GWAS/co-expression analyses.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is negatively correlated with starch content in flag leaves at heading stage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flag leaves at the heading stage; part of co-regulated Cluster 2 in hotspot L01.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: eQTL mapping; GWAS co-localization; expression-phenotype correlation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os01g02430 is one of four co-regulated sn-glycerol 3-phosphate synthesis-related genes linked to starch-content QTL LS01. 2. Its eQTL overlaps starch GWAS association. 3. Increased expression is associated with reduced starch content in flag leaves.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct perturbation experiment was performed for this gene in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to ent-copalyl diphosphate, providing specialized ent-CPP precursor for diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key biosynthetic gene for diterpenoid antitoxin production and is positively regulated by bHLH026.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; RT-qPCR in bHLH026 knockout/overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS2 is identified as a downstream target of bHLH026 in HS002-associated diterpenoid metabolism. 2. bHLH026 binds the CPS2 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. CPS2 is described as a key gene for production of specialized ent-CPP in rice diterpene metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves; transcript reduced in bhlh026-cri and elevated in bHLH026-OX.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heading stage; transgenic seedlings for metabolite profiling; Xoo-related defense context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be co-engineered with bHLH026 or other diterpene-pathway genes to enhance phytoalexin accumulation and disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct gene manipulation of CPS2 itself was not performed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ent-CPP or syn-CPP-derived copalyl diphosphate intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts copalyl diphosphate-derived precursor into branch-specific diterpene scaffold for rice diterpenoid antitoxins.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class I diterpene synthase / kaurene synthase-like
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthesis as a downstream gene activated by bHLH026.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in bHLH026 knockout/overexpression lines; hotspot/co-expression/eQTL analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KSL5 is among the downstream genes in HS002 linked to diterpenoid antitoxin synthesis. 2. Its expression follows bHLH026 activity in transgenic lines. 3. It likely contributes to branch-specific diterpene scaffold formation for phytoalexin production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed downstream gene of bHLH026; lower in knockout and higher in overexpression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: transgenic manipulation of bHLH026; defense-related context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No promoter-binding assay for KSL5 was reported.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts copalyl diphosphate-derived substrate into a diterpene scaffold used for rice diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class I diterpene synthase / kaurene synthase-like
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream biosynthetic gene for diterpenoid antitoxin synthesis activated by bHLH026.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; RT-qPCR in bHLH026 transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KSL6 is a direct transcriptional target of bHLH026. 2. bHLH026 binds the KSL6 promoter and activates its expression. 3. KSL6 expression is reduced in bhlh026-cri and increased in bHLH026-OX lines.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves; positively correlated with bHLH026 activity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: transgenic manipulation of bHLH026; defense-related context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used together with CPS2 promoter in direct binding and activation assays.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out oxidative modification of diterpene scaffold intermediates in rice diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downstream biosynthetic gene positively associated with bHLH026-mediated diterpenoid antitoxin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in bHLH026 transgenic lines; hotspot/co-expression/eQTL analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP701A8 is listed as a downstream gene of bHLH026. 2. It participates in diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthesis through oxidative tailoring. 3. Its expression is promoted by functional bHLH026.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed downstream gene responsive to bHLH026.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic product not detailed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative tailoring of diterpene intermediates toward rice diterpenoid antitoxins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream biosynthetic gene associated with bHLH026-controlled diterpenoid antitoxin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in bHLH026 transgenic lines; hotspot/co-expression/eQTL analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71Z6 is a downstream gene of bHLH026 identified in HS002. 2. It is associated with diterpenoid antitoxin synthesis. 3. Its transcript abundance responds positively to bHLH026 activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed downstream gene responsive to bHLH026.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific substrate/product pair not provided in this article.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator/master regulator in distant eQTL hotspot HS002 that activates multiple diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CPS2; KSL6; KSL5; cytochrome P450 701A8; cytochrome P450 71Z6
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates downstream diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthetic genes; directly binds and activates CPS2 and KSL6 promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not established; expression is influenced by local eQTL variation linked to downstream distant eQTL signals.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: local eQTL variants including lead SNP vg1015145775; haplotype variation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes diterpenoid antitoxin synthesis and enhances resistance to Xanthomonas oryzae; loss of function reduces downstream gene expression and disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; eQTL and linkage analysis; haplotype analysis; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; Xoo infection assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH026 was identified from distant eQTL hotspot HS002 as a candidate master regulator co-expressed with diterpenoid antitoxin biosynthetic genes. 2. It has local eQTLs, and its lead SNPs are linked to distant eQTLs of downstream genes. 3. bHLH026 directly binds CPS2 and KSL6 promoters and activates transcription. 4. Overexpression increases multiple diterpenoid antitoxins and intermediates, whereas knockout reduces expression of downstream genes and some metabolites. 5. bHLH026 overexpression increases resistance to Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae, while knockout increases susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves/flag leaves at heading stage; haplotype-dependent expression differences, with Hap1 higher than Hap4 in japonica.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heading stage; pathogen infection by Xoo strain Zhe134; sterile seedling growth for metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rice diterpenoid antitoxins/phytoalexins contribute to broad-spectrum disease resistance, including resistance to Xanthomonas oryzae.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA858547; metabolomics by LC-ESI-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Knockout lines showed weaker metabolite decreases than overexpression-induced increases for many compounds, likely because basal diterpenoid antitoxin levels are low in wild type and knockout. Haplotypes show subspecies-biased distribution (Hap1/Hap4 in japonica; Hap2/Hap3 in indica/Aus).
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering or breeding rice with enhanced phytoalexin accumulation and bacterial blight resistance; may be combined with downstream diterpene pathway genes for stronger metabolic output.
